chapter:14088.0.briefing: Tornadoes pose catastrophic risk across your territory with exposure nearly double the national median, and Scott County faces the highest threat at 96.9 out of 100. chapter:18206.0.briefing: Hurricane exposure is catastrophic across your territory with an average risk score of 97 out of 100—nearly double the U.S. median—and St. Mary Parish faces the perfect storm at 98.4 hurricane risk while 43.4% of residents struggle financially. St. Mary alone represents your highest convergence of climate and economic vulnerability, demanding immediate preparedness investment before the next storm season.

chapter:22322.0.briefing: Strong wind poses an extreme threat across your territory with Wayne County scoring 99.7 out of 100—nearly off the charts—and driving significant portions of the region's $1.2 billion expected annual loss. Wayne County also has your highest economic vulnerability at 36% of residents struggling, meaning wind events will hit families with the least capacity to recover.

chapter:24277.0.briefing: Hurricane exposure in George County demands immediate attention, with a catastrophic risk score of 98.2 out of 100—among the highest in your entire 28-county territory along the Gulf Coast. More than 40% of your region's population is financially fragile, meaning a single storm could push thousands of families already living paycheck-to-paycheck into crisis without pre-positioned shelter, cash assistance, and recovery resources.

ARC of Southeast Missouri and Northeast Arkansas

chapter:25060.briefing: Extreme heat is slamming this chapter at near-maximum intensity with Iron County scoring in the 99.8th percentile nationally, threatening a population where 40% are already financially vulnerable—10 points above the US median. Reynolds County combines that heat exposure with the highest overall disaster risk in your entire 18-county territory at 99.5, meaning climate and economic fragility are colliding in the same communities.

scope.name: ARC of Southeast Missouri and Northeast Arkansas
ARC of Southeast Missouri and Northeast Arkansas

chapter:25060.0.briefing: Earthquake exposure in Mississippi County and across the New Madrid Seismic Zone dwarfs typical American Red Cross chapters, with risk scores hitting 98 out of 100 compared to a national median of 65. More than half of residents in St. Francis County are economically fragile, meaning disaster recovery capacity is nearly nonexistent without pre-positioned resources and external aid.

chapter:27234.briefing: Nance County faces catastrophic tornado exposure with a near-perfect risk score of 99 out of 100, making it your most urgent preparedness and response priority. This small county also ranks as your most vulnerable overall at 99, meaning limited local capacity will be overwhelmed when—not if—the next major tornado strikes.
